chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 11 of 2022 C. P. No. D – 12 of 2022 C. P. No. D – 163 of 2022 C. P. No. D – 306 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge C. P. No. D-11 of 2022 Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of CMA No.33/2022 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case C. P. No. D-12 of 2022 Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of CMA No.36/2022 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case C. P. No. D-163 of 2022 Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of CMA No.774/2022 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case C. P. No. D-306 of 2022 Hearing of case (priority) 1. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 2. For hearing of CMA No.1311/2022 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 14.09.2023 M/s Fayaz Ali Metlo and Liaquat Ali Metlo, Advocates for petitioner in C. Ps. No. D-11 & 12 of 2022. Mr. Muhammad Habib Khan, Advocate for respondents No.3 & 4 in C. Ps. No. D-11 & 12 of 2022. Mr. Saeed Ahmed Baloch, Advocate for respondents (Evacuee Trust Property) in C. P. No. D-306 of 2022. Mr. Ashfaque Hussain Abro, Assistant Attorney General..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- C. Ps. No. D-11 & 12 of 2022: Learned Counsel for respondents No.3 & 4 (Evacuee Trust Property) has filed a statement along with copy of order passed by Additional Secretary (Incharge), Ministry of Religious Affairs and C. P. No. D – 11 of 2022 & others Page 2 of 2 Interfaith Harmony, Islamabad, whereby Revision Petitions No.3-01 & 02/2022-Rev filed by the petitioner have been decided in the terms whereby the impugned order has been set aside and the cases have been remanded to the Chairman, ETPB for de novo consideration as per law. This statement and copy of the order are taken on record.

D – 11 of 2022 & others Page 2 of 2 Interfaith Harmony, Islamabad, whereby Revision Petitions No.3-01 & 02/2022-Rev filed by the petitioner have been decided in the terms whereby the impugned order has been set aside and the cases have been remanded to the Chairman, ETPB for de novo consideration as per law. This statement and copy of the order are taken on record. In view of above, both petitions (C. Ps. No. D-11 & 12 of 2022), having become infructuous, are disposed of along with listed applications, with consent of all parties. C. Ps. No. D-163 & 306 of 2022: Mr. Israr Ahmed Shah, Advocate holding brief for Mr. Rukhsar Ahmed M. Junejo, Advocate for petitioners, requests for adjournment on the ground that latter is busy before another Bench of this Court. Adjourned to 26th October 2023. Office is directed to de-tag files of these petitions from disposed of C. Ps. No. D-11 & 12 of 2022. Office is directed to place a signed copy of this order in the captioned connected matters. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
